card_page.policy_text: Library Cards | Watertown Library, MA - Official Website Skip to Main Content Contact Get Email Updates Get a Card My Account Free and open to all. Watertown, Massachusetts. Catalog Search Website Search Catalog Search Website Search Catalog Search Website Search Search the Website Type 3 or more characters to see suggestions Use up/down arrow keys to navigate suggestions, Enter to select, Esc to close. About Expand About Submenu Collections Expand Collections Submenu Services Expand Services Submenu Events Expand Events Submenu Project Literacy Expand Project Literacy Submenu Opens in a new window Home Services Borrowing Library Cards Library Cards Library Cards & Borrowing Account Management Library Cards & Borrowing Account Management Library Cards & Borrowing Getting a Library Card Anyone who lives in Massachusetts can get a library card at the Watertown Free Public Library. How to Apply Apply in person in the library, or apply online . Identification Requirements Before creating a new patron record, the individual must present the following: Current, valid Massachusetts Driver’s license or Massachusetts State Identification Card with current address. OR One document from Section 1 AND one document from Section 2 below. 1. Proof of identification –current or expired-- (must include name and photo) Passport Alien Resident Card Government issued cards, such as Military ID State issued photo ID including welfare, Medicaid or FID card University or school ID Senior/T.A.P. ID issued by the MBTA (added by Membership 01/18/2017) 2. Proof of current local address* (must include applicant’s name) Utility bill or tax bill (dated within the last 60 days) Lease agreement Imprinted bank check or deposit slip Official school schedule with applicant’s name and address typed on it Official letter verifying residency and mailing address dated within last 30 days from a social service provider, temporary employer that provides housing, or a short-term residence Postcard mailed to applicant from the library Personal letter mailed to the applicant, postmarked within 60 days eBills * A Post Office Box or business address is not sufficient – a current residential address is required. Notes: For patrons who cannot produce photo identification or proof of current local address, someone in an official capacity may attest to a person’s identification and/or address (e.g., someone who works in an official capacity at a shelter to attest to the identification and/or address of a person). No longer acceptable as proof of current address: rent receipts. Children If applicant is under 13 years old, parent or legal guardian must provide proof of identification and address. A child must be present for a parent or legal guardian to get them a library card. Teens If applicant is between the ages of 13 and 17 and cannot meet the ID and address requirements, parent or guardian must provide proof of identification and address. A teen must be present for a parent or legal guardian to get them a library card. Out-of-State Residents Out-of-state Residents who meet the following requirements, may be issued a library card with sufficient identification: Work in Massachusetts Attend school in Massachusetts Own property in Massachusetts Are temporarily living in Massachusetts for more than 2 months Out of state residents must provide identification showing their home/permanent address. Property owners must provide verification such as tax bill, utility bill, etc for their Massachusetts property. Students must verify their status with school identification and provide their school address. Out-of-state residents working in Massachusetts must provide their work address. Patrons who are temporary residents (such as college students or au pairs) should be registered with permanent address in the secondary address field. Reporting a Lost Card You can report a lost card by phone or in person. Until a card is reported lost, the cardholder is responsible for all materials checked out to the card, including any items that have been claimed to have been returned. Expired Library Card Library cards may be renewed in person, online , or over the phone. To update your library account status, you will need to have your expired library card ready. You will also need your password if renewing online. Account Management Your Online Library Account Have you set up your account online? If not, be sure to do so if you'd like to: Renew items online Find and request items Manage email preferences View the status of your requests Save catalog searches Modify your personal information Setting Up Your Account To set up your account, you just need your library card handy! Go to the "My Account" login page . If this is your first time logging on, leave the section for "password" blank. You'll create one in the next step. Click submit, choose a password, and you've now got access to your account all day, every day! The next time you want to login to your account, all you have to do is visit the catalog page and click "Login to My Account" in the upper right hand corner. Placing Holds Online Library cardholders may place a hold on materials through the Minuteman Library Catalog. Each patron may have up to 50 holds at one time. When placing items on hold, the length of time it takes to arrive can vary. If you are number one on the holds list and there are copies of the item on the shelf when the hold is placed, the usual time is 3 to 4 days for the item to be ready for pick up. If many people are waiting for the same item which you request, it may take several weeks to receive the item. Once a hold arrives, we will hold it for 7 days. A phone call, email or text message notification is used, whichever the patron has selected for use, once the item is available for pick up. Paying Library Fines You can pay your fines through your MLN account. Log in to My Account . Click on the link that says "fines." Catalog Library Cards Borrow & Return Home Delivery Reading Recommendations Adults Anti-Racist Reading & Resources For Adults Teens Children Suggest a Purchase My Account Log in for more Room Reservations Book a meeting or study room Museum Passes Browse passes online eLibrary View our digital services Library of Things Browse things Ask a Librarian Submit questions Contact Us Watertown Free Public Library 123 Main Street Watertown, Massachusetts 02472 Phone: 617-972-6431 Submit Feedback Hours Monday – Thursday: 9 AM – 9 PM Friday: 9 AM – 7 PM Saturday: 9 AM – 5 PM Sunday: 1 PM – 5 pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "65 Main Street, Watertown, MA 02472","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-armenian-museum-of-america_452_md.png","telephone": "617-926-2562","name": "Armenian Museum of America","url": "","description": "With roots going back to the early 1970s, The Armenian Museum of America has occupied its Watertown location since 1988. The largest collection of Armenian cultural artifacts outside of Armenia, the 25,000 objects include five of the first printed bibles in Armenian from 1666, ancient Urartian artifacts, medieval manuscripts, K�tahya ceramics, textiles, liturgical objects, and contemporary art. With such a large collection, the museum estimates that only around 5% of its holding are on view at a given time, through rotating and permanent exhibits, including explorations of modern day Armenian-American culture, artworks by contemporary Armenian and Armenian-American artists, and an exhibit focusing on the Armenian Genocide. All galleries are wheelchair accessible, and there are HEPA Air Purifiers throughout. There is a gift shop featuring work by Armenian vendors."} Armenian Museum of America 65 Main Street, Watertown, MA 02472617-926-2562https://www.armenianmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Armenian Museum of America With roots going back to the early 1970s, The Armenian Museum of America has occupied its Watertown location since 1988. The largest collection of Armenian cultural artifacts outside of Armenia, the 25,000 objects include five of the first printed bibles in Armenian from 1666, ancient Urartian artifacts, medieval manuscripts, K�tahya ceramics, textiles, liturgical objects, and contemporary art. With such a large collection, the museum estimates that only around 5% of its holding are on view at a given time, through rotating and permanent exhibits, including explorations of modern day Armenian-American culture, artworks by contemporary Armenian and Armenian-American artists, and an exhibit focusing on the Armenian Genocide. All galleries are wheelchair accessible, and there are HEPA Air Purifiers throughout. There is a gift shop featuring work by Armenian vendors.
Categories: Art, Crafts, Family, History
Pass Type: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
Pass Benefits This pass discount provides free admission for 1 individual. This coupon pass must be picked up in person at the Library's Circulation desk.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits This pass discount provides half priced admission for up to 4 individuals including children. Timed ticket reservations must be made in advance of visit with library e-voucher code on the museum's website. Directions and information regarding e-voucher use will be provided in your confirmation email. 
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-harbor-islands_6_md.jpg","telephone": "617-223-8666","name": "Boston Harbor Island Ferry","url": "","description": "The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Harbor Island Ferry The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
Categories: Family, Nature, Recreation
Pass Benefits The pass provides 50% off up to 4 ferry tickets from Boston to Spectacle Island and Georges Island or from Hingham to Peddocks Island.
This is a SEASONAL pass available mid-May through early October.
Georges Island*May 15 - June 14: Thursdays through Sundays*June 16 - September 1: daily
Spectacle Island*July 15 - September 1: daily*September 4 - September 21: Thursdays through Sundays*September 26 - October 12: Fridays through Sundays
Peddocks Island from Hingham*June 15  September 7: every day EXCEPT Tuesdays*September 10 - October 12: Thursdays through Sundays
The most up-to-date information can be found at: www.bostonharborislands.org
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-discovery-museums_4_md.jpg","telephone": "978-264-4200","name": "Discovery Museum","url": "","description": "Acton&apos;s Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum&apos;s building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles. "} Discovery Museum 177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720978-264-4200http://www.discoveryacton.org/[Details/Reserve]
Discovery Museum Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
Categories: Children, Family, Science
Pass Benefits The pass discount provides half priced admissions for up to 3 individuals including children. Directions and information regarding e-voucher use will be provided in your confirmation email. 
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "180 Hemenway Road, Framingham, MA 01701","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-native-plant-trust_12_md.jpg","telephone": "508-877-7630","name": "Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust)","url": "","description": "The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public."} Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) 180 Hemenway Road, Framingham, MA 01701508-877-7630http://www.nativeplanttrust.org/[Details/Reserve]
Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public.
Categories: Family, Nature
Pass Type: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
